subtitle: Bilingual edition · 双语对照版

intro.text: 今天 1 个 podcast + 13 条 X + 3 篇 blog，主轴意外是物理这一层——MAD Podcast 把 OpenAI 的 Head of Industrial Compute、Sachin Khalti（前 Stanford 教授、前 Intel CTO）请来，把「史上最大基建」一次性讲完。数据中心是「把电子变成智能」的工厂，液冷要做到每一层（连传输电力的 transformer 都要冷），电网不够就直接投资发电和输电；Jalapeno 自研芯片把 per-watt tokens 当核心优化指标，因为电成了 bottleneck——近处的游戏是人类工程师把 GW 级电送上线，再为已知 workload co-design 硅。Anthropic Engineering 同一天连发两篇把执行层这件事讲完：April-23 post-mortem 追到三处独立改动撞到一起（Claude Code 默认 reasoning effort 降级 / idle session 每轮都清旧 thinking / 减啰嗦 system prompt 伤了 coding），关键教训是三次小 drift 看起来像一次大 regression；Managed Agents 那篇是 OS 类比——把 session / harness / sandbox 三件事虚拟化、互相解耦，harness 编码了对「Claude 自己不能做什么」的假设，但那些假设会过期，所以接口要 design 到活得比任何具体实现更久。Claude Blog 把 execution 这块继续展开：self-hosted sandboxes + MCP tunnels 让 code execution、敏感文件、packages 全留在企业边界里，agent loop 仍然在 Anthropic infra 上跑。OpenAI 这一头：Thibault 周末把 Codex + ChatGPT Work 所有付费用户的 usage limits 全部 reset，并暗示可能顺手 reset 了别处的 rate limits——GPT-5.6 Sol 的需求信号强到这一侧愿意周末把 rate-limit 闸门放掉。Claude 官方账号周末把 Fable 5 的供给侧调整讲清楚：7 月 20 日起 Fable 5 进 Max / Team Premium（50% of limits），Pro / Team Standard 拿到一次性 $100 credit——承认 Fable 需求「challenging to predict」之后才稳定下来。AEO / SEO 这一块 swyx 把同一条线讲三遍：每周末让 agent 自动研究怎么提升自家排名是免费 alpha，alpha 只在 meta-conversation 转到 on-policy auto-AEO 那天才消失。Peter Yang 报告 Codex browser use 终于被击败；Steipete 把 Codex 当 build partner 用出花（macOS picker 自己点 / 让 Codex 自己写 Codexbar 的 icon editor）；Rauch 把 Vercel Sandbox 的下载功能免费出来，Levie 把「AI 越便宜 TAM 越大」的逻辑钉出来。Madhu Guru 把企业做不出 chatbot 之外 agent 的真相讲完——harness + eval 的人才 gap，Anthropic 的抽象层真正在争取的客户就是这一批「三件都没有」的企业；Thariq 把 prototype-first 的心得压成一条 tweet，跟 Granola 的 shaping→validate→make it good 是同一个 shape。Weekend texture 由 Zara Zhang「会议现在默认被录下来给 agent 用」、Nikunj 5:32am 的父子对话、Amasad 给 Replit 社区的 ChessMaxxing 命名收尾。

sections.title: MAD Podcast：OpenAI 工业算力总管 Sachin 把「物理世界跟不上 AI 节奏」讲完 / MAD Podcast: OpenAI's Head of Industrial Compute on the Physical Reality of the AI Buildout
sections.desc: Matt Turck 这期把 OpenAI 的 Head of Industrial Compute、Sachin Khalti（前 Stanford 教授、前 Intel CTO）请来，整期主线：这场「史上最大基建」是 AI 撞上物理世界，需求永远比供给快——你以为算力够了，过几个月才发现早该继续扩。数据中心是「把电子变成智能」的工厂，液冷是必须的（连传输电力的变压器都得冷），电网不够就直接投资发电和输电基础设施。OpenAI 自研的 Jalapeno 芯片把「per-watt tokens」当核心优化指标，因为电成了 bottleneck；硬件-模型 co-design 才是下一步 scaling 的关键 ingredient。

cards.authorTag: Matt Turck 主理的 AI 与数据播客

rewrite: The MAD Podcast, this week: Matt Turck sits down with Sachin Khalti (Head of Industrial Compute at OpenAI, ex-Stanford professor, ex-CTO of Intel). The central thread: this is one of the largest infrastructure builds in human history, and the physical world doesn't move as fast as AI demand does.

On the demand-supply gap: 'Anytime you have thought you have enough compute, we can slow down. Always negatively surprises like, oh, we should not have slowed down. Demand far outstrips compute supply today. So anything we can bring online, we consume immediately.' Sachin's worry is still the scale of trying to get compute and build compute — the moment you stop building, the regret compounds.

On data centers as 'factories turning electrons into intelligence': 'How do we take those electrons and actually use it to power chips that effectively are delivering intelligence? The way I visualize it is large football fields, liquid gold because these chips run really hot. The temperatures on these chips are very, very high.' Cooling has to happen at every layer — data halls, individual chips, and even the cables and transformers distributing power.

On the grid: 'At this point, we are beginning to hit and we are investing in generation infrastructure for the grid, transmission infrastructure for the grid.' Wherever OpenAI builds a data center, it commits to not taking power away from the grid — in fact, it's funding the development of generation + transmission that wouldn't otherwise have been funded. Where the grid can't scale further, the data center goes 'behind the meter' with on-site generation, currently gas turbines, with nuclear as the long-term answer.

So anything we can bring online, we consume immediately. Our biggest worry is that still at the scale at which we are trying to get compute and build compute.
The physical world does not move that fast. We do believe that the world of recursion is not that far where AI will design the systems it needs to train and run the next generation of AI. Encourages.

It's a popular phrase nowadays, but it actually has a lot of a ring of truth to it. So how do we take power? How do we take those electrons and actually use it to power chips that effectively are delivering intelligence? And the way I visualize it is large football fields, liquid gold because these chips run really hot. The temperatures on these chips are very, very high.

I think the early days, we all connected to the grid, and we still all would want to connect to the grid. At this point, we are beginning to hit and we are investing in generation infrastructure for the grid, transmission infrastructure for the grid. So whenever we build a data center anywhere, we make it a hard commitment that we are not taking power away from the grid. In fact, we are investing

You have a grid somewhere. Has a certain generation and distribution capability, certain number of megawatts. Obviously, data center shows up. If there was spare capacity, then of course, the data center can use it. But if there isn't spare capacity, then we have to new gas or solar or hydro generation infrastructure to the grid.

rewrite: MAD Podcast 这期把 OpenAI 的 Head of Industrial Compute、Sachin Khalti（前 Stanford 教授、前 Intel CTO）请来。整期主线：这场「史上最大基建」是 AI 撞上物理世界的故事，物理世界不会像模型那样跟着 scale。
需求 vs 供给：「任何时候你觉得算力够用了可以慢下来，事后看总是后悔——当时不该慢。需求远超供给，我们能上线多少就立刻吃掉多少。」Sachin 反复强调的焦虑是：你以为够的那一刹，已经晚了几个月。
把数据中心想象成「把电子变成智能的工厂」：「怎么把电接到 chip 上、让 chip 实际在产出智能？我脑子里的画面是一整片足球场那么大的液冷黄金——chip 跑得很烫，温度非常高。」液冷要做到每一层：机房级、chip 级，连走电的 cable 和 transformer 都要冷，因为处理能量就产生热量。

跟电网的关系：「早期我们都接电网，今天也仍然想接电网。但现在已经触到天花板了，所以我们在投发电基础设施、投输电基础设施。」OpenAI 在哪儿建数据中心，都先做一个 hard commitment：不从电网抢电——反过来，他们主动给电网投钱，建本来不会被建的发电+输电设施。当电网本身扩不动的时候，数据中心就『behind the meter』自建发电，目前主要是 gas turbine，长期方向是 nuclear。
original: 任何时候你觉得算力够用、可以慢下来，回头看总是后悔——当时不该慢。今天需求远超供给，我们能上线多少就立刻吃掉多少。我们最大的焦虑是，要拿到、要建出这么多算力的 scale 仍然不够。
物理世界不会移动得那么快。我们相信「递归的世界」已经不远了——AI 会设计出它自己需要的系统，去训练和跑下一代的 AI。
这是句流行话，但其实很有道理。怎么把电拿到、怎么把电子接到 chip 上、让 chip 真正在产出智能？我脑子里的画面是一片足球场那么大的液冷黄金——chip 跑得很烫，温度非常高。
早期我们都接电网，今天也想接电网。但现在已经触到天花板了，所以我们开始投资发电、投输电。我们在哪儿建数据中心，都做一个 hard commitment：不从电网抢电，反而要往电网里投钱。
一个地方有一张电网，有一定的发电和配电能力，有若干兆瓦。数据中心落下来，如果电网有富余容量，数据中心当然可以用；但如果没有富余容量，我们就得新建 gas / solar / hydro 的发电设施接到这张电网上。

cards.authorTag: Matt Turck 主理的 AI 与数据播客

rewrite: On the Jalapeno custom silicon program: 'Because we know what is the workload exactly, what is the model we want to run, we can co-design the hardware to be super efficient in delivering those models. So it really allows us to drive efficiency advantage, drive more tokens per watt.' Inference is becoming a big fraction of OpenAI's workload, and once you know the workload, you can squeeze more out of every joule.

The metric that matters: 'The key metric that Jalapeno is optimizing is maximizing the number of tokens you can produce per watt. And because the world is constrained by power today, so the more tokens you can produce for the same number on on power, it's better for everyone.' Tokens-per-watt is the new FLOPS — a power-constrained world makes efficiency the dominant axis.

The closing thought: AI will eventually design the systems it needs to train and run the next generation. Sachin frames it as 'the world of recursion is not that far' — but the near-term game is still human engineers bringing gigawatts online, then co-designing silicon for known workloads. The industrial layer and the model layer are now the same layer.
original: As we begin to as we serve a pretty big fraction of the world's population, AI usage is exploding, inference is obviously becoming a big fraction of our workload. Right? It's consuming a lot of compute. And one of the other realizations is because we know what is the workload exactly, what is the model we want to run, we can co design the hardware to be super efficient in delivering those models.

So it really allows us to drive efficiency advantage, drive more tokens per watt. So the key metric that Jalapeno is optimizing is maximizing the number of tokens you can produce per watt. And because the world is constrained by power today, so the more tokens you can produce for the same number on on power, it's better for everyone. So we look at it as a very critical ingredient in scaling how we

rewrite: 聊 OpenAI 的 Jalapeno 自研芯片：「因为我们已经清楚 workload 是什么、要跑什么模型，就可以把硬件和模型 co-design，做到超级高效。」Inference 已经是 OpenAI workload 里越来越大的一块，一旦你彻底清楚 workload，硬件效率就能做到极限。
核心 metric：「Jalapeno 优化的核心指标是 per-watt tokens——每瓦电你能产出多少 token。因为今天世界被 power 卡住了，每瓦多产 token 对大家都是好事。」tokens-per-watt 是新的 FLOPS——电被卡住的世界里，效率就是 dominant axis。
收尾思想：AI 最终会自己设计出训练和跑下一代 AI 所需的系统。Sachin 把这叫「递归的世界已经不远」——但近处的游戏仍然是人类工程师把 GW 级的电送上线，再为已知的 workload co-design 硅。工业层和模型层，已经是同一层。
original: 当我们服务全球相当大一部分人口的时候，AI 用量在爆炸，inference 显然变成了我们 workload 里的一个大块。另一个 realization 是：因为我们彻底清楚 workload 是什么、要跑什么模型，我们就可以把硬件和模型一起 co-design，做到超级高效。
这让我们拿到效率优势——每瓦电产出更多 token。Jalapeno 优化的核心指标就是每瓦能产出多少 token，因为今天世界被 power 卡住了，每瓦能多产 token 对所有人都更好。所以我们把它当成 scale 路上非常关键的一味 ingredient。
我们相信「递归的世界」并不遥远——到那时候，AI 会自己设计它需要的系统，去训练和跑下一代的 AI。

sections.title: Anthropic Engineering：Claude Code 体验回退 post-mortem，三处独立改动撞到一起 / Anthropic Engineering: Claude Code Quality Post-Mortem — Three Independent Changes Collided
sections.desc: Anthropic Engineering 这篇是诚实的 post-mortem：过去一个月用户报的「Claude 变笨」，最终追到三处独立改动分别撞到 Claude Code、Agent SDK、Cowork 三块产品（API 完全没受影响）。三件事各自时间线不同、影响切片不同，所以叠加效果看起来像 broad、inconsistent 的退化。March 4 把 Claude Code 默认 reasoning effort 从 high 降到 medium（April 7 退回）、March 26 给 idle session 清旧 thinking 时出了 bug 每轮都清（April 10 修）、April 16 加 system prompt 减啰嗦反而伤了 coding 质量（April 20 撤）。关键教训：3 月就开始收到反馈，但跟用户自然反馈噪声难分辨。

rewrite: Anthropic Engineering's April-23 post-mortem traces 'Claude responses have worsened' reports to three independent changes — not model degradation. 'We never intentionally degrade our models, and we were able to immediately confirm that our API and inference layer were unaffected.' The API was untouched; the degradation came from product-layer changes.

Issue 1 (March 4): Claude Code's default reasoning effort was changed from high to medium to fix long-latency UX in high mode. 'This was the wrong tradeoff. We reverted this change on April 7 after users told us they'd prefer to default to higher intelligence and opt into lower effort for simple tasks.' Affected Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.6.

Issue 2 (March 26): a session-management change was meant to clear Claude's older thinking once when an idle session resumed. 'A bug caused this to keep happening every turn for the rest of the session instead of just once, which made Claude seem forgetful and repetitive.' Fixed April 10. Same two models affected.

Issue 3 (April 16): a system prompt instruction to reduce verbosity 'in combination with other prompt changes, it hurt coding quality and was reverted on April 20. This impacted Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.6, and Opus 4.7.' Broader scope than the first two.

The closing lesson: 'Because each change affected a different slice of traffic on a different schedule, the aggregate effect looked like broad, inconsistent degradation. While we began investigating reports in early March, they were challenging to distinguish from normal variation in user feedback.' A useful pattern to internalize for any product that ships prompt-level changes incrementally — three small drifts look like one big regression.
original: Over the past month, we've been looking into reports that Claude's responses have worsened for some users. We've traced these reports to three separate changes that affected Claude Code, the Claude Agent SDK, and Claude Cowork. The API was not impacted. All three issues have now been resolved as of April 20 (v2.1.116). In this post, we explain what we found, what we fixed, and what we'll do differently to ensure similar issues are much less likely to happen again.

We take reports about degradation very seriously. We never intentionally degrade our models, and we were able to immediately confirm that our API and inference layer were unaffected. After investigation, we identified three different issues: On March 4, we changed Claude Code's default reasoning effort from high to medium to reduce the very long latency—enough to make the UI appear frozen—some users were seeing in high mode.

On March 26, we shipped a change to clear Claude's older thinking from sessions that had been idle for over an hour, to reduce latency when users resumed those sessions. A bug caused this to keep happening every turn for the rest of the session instead of just once, which made Claude seem forgetful and repetitive.

On April 16, we added a system prompt instruction to reduce verbosity. In combination with other prompt changes, it hurt coding quality and was reverted on April 20. This impacted Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.6, and Opus 4.7.

Because each change affected a different slice of traffic on a different schedule, the aggregate effect looked like broad, inconsistent degradation. While we began investigating reports in early March, they were challenging to distinguish from normal variation in user feedbac

rewrite: Anthropic Engineering 这篇 April-23 post-mortem 把「Claude 变笨」的报告追到三处独立改动——不是模型主动退化。「我们从不主动降低模型表现，能立刻确认 API 和 inference 层完全没受影响。」API 没动；退化来自产品层的 prompt/harness 改动。
Issue 1（3 月 4 日）：Claude Code 的默认 reasoning effort 从 high 降到 medium，为了解决 high 模式下「UI 看起来卡死」的延迟问题。「这个 tradeoff 是错的，4 月 7 日我们已经退回。用户的反馈很清楚：默认走更高智能、要降的时候自己手动选。」影响 Sonnet 4.6 和 Opus 4.6。
Issue 2（3 月 26 日）：session 管理原本设计为「idle session 恢复时清一次旧 thinking」。「一个 bug 让这件事变成每轮都清，所以 Claude 显得很健忘、很重复。」4 月 10 日修。同一批两个模型。
Issue 3（4 月 16 日）：加了一条让 Claude 少啰嗦的 system prompt instruction。「这条改动跟其他 prompt 改动叠加之后伤了 coding 质量，4 月 20 日撤。」影响 Sonnet 4.6、Opus 4.6、Opus 4.7——比前两个范围更广。
收尾教训：「三处改动各自影响不同 traffic 切片、时间线不同，叠加之后看起来像 broad、inconsistent 的退化。3 月初就开始收到反馈，但跟用户的自然反馈噪声很难区分。」对任何 incremental 发 prompt 改动的产品都值得内化这条：三次小 drift 看起来像一次大 regression。
original: 过去一个月里，我们一直在追「Claude 表现变差」的用户报告。最终追到三处独立改动，分别撞到 Claude Code、Claude Agent SDK 和 Claude Cowork 三块产品。API 完全没受影响。所有三处问题在 4 月 20 日（v2.1.116）都已修复。这篇里我们说清楚发现了什么、改了什么、之后会怎样降低类似事件重发的概率。
我们对「退化」的报告很严肃。我们从不主动降低模型表现，能立刻确认 API 和 inference 层完全没受影响。调查后识别出三处不同的问题：3 月 4 日，我们把 Claude Code 的默认 reasoning effort 从 high 改成 medium，为了缓解 high 模式下用户看到的「UI 看起来卡死」的过长延迟。
3 月 26 日，我们发了一个改动，用来在 idle 一小时以上的 session 被恢复时清掉 Claude 的旧 thinking。Bug 让这件事变成整个 session 后续每轮都清，而不是只清一次——所以 Claude 看起来很健忘、很重复。
4 月 16 日，我们加了一条减少啰嗦的 system prompt instruction。它跟其他 prompt 改动叠加之后伤了 coding 质量，4 月 20 日已撤回。这次影响了 Sonnet 4.6、Opus 4.6 和 Opus 4.7。
因为三处改动各自影响不同切片、时间线不同，叠加之后看起来像 broad、inconsistent 的退化。3 月初我们就开始调查反馈，但跟用户正常反馈噪声很难分辨。

sections.title: Anthropic Engineering：Managed Agents 把 session / harness / sandbox 拆开，类比 OS 把硬件虚拟化 / Anthropic Engineering: Managed Agents Decouple Session / Harness / Sandbox, an OS-Style Abstraction Layer
sections.desc: 同一支 Engineering 团队的下一篇：Managed Agents 是 Claude Platform 上的 hosted service，把 agent 拆成三件事——session（append-only log）、harness（调用 Claude + 路由 tool calls 的 loop）、sandbox（执行环境）。三件事分别可替换，类比 OS 把硬件虚拟化成 process / file 这种足够通用的抽象，让「还没被想出来的程序」也能跑。harness 编码了对「Claude 自己不能做什么」的假设，但那些假设会随模型变强而过期——Managed Agents 的接口设计就是为了扛过任何具体实现，包括今天 Anthropic 自己在跑的。

rewrite: Anthropic Engineering's Managed Agents post builds an OS-style argument for how to design systems for 'programs as yet unthought of.' 'Decades ago, operating systems solved this problem by virtualizing hardware into abstractions—process, file—general enough for programs that didn't exist yet. The abstractions outlasted the hardware. The read() command is agnostic as to whether it's accessing a disk pack from the 1970s or a modern SSD.'

Managed Agents follow the same pattern. 'We virtualized the components of an agent: a session (the append-only log of everything that happened), a harness (the loop that calls Claude and routes Claude's tool calls to the relevant infrastructure), and a sandbox (an execution environment where Claude can run code and edit files). This allows the implementation of each to be swapped without disturbing the others.'

On why harnesses need to be revisitable: 'A common thread across this work is that harnesses encode assumptions about what Claude can't do on its own. However, those assumptions need to be frequently questioned because they can go stale as models improve.' Concrete example: Claude Sonnet 4.5 had 'context anxiety' (wrapping up prematurely as it sensed context limits), so the harness added context resets. When the same harness ran on Claude Opus 4.5, the behavior was gone — the resets had become dead weight.

The implication: 'We expect harnesses to continue evolving. So we built Managed Agents: a hosted service in the Claude Platform that runs long-horizon agents on your behalf through a small set of interfaces meant to outlast any particular implementation—including the ones we run today.' The interfaces are the contract; the implementations underneath are replaceable. Same playbook as a 1970s OS calling read() without knowing what's on the other end.
original: Get started with Claude Managed Agents by following our docs. A running topic on the Engineering Blog is how to build effective agents and design harnesses for long-running work.

A common thread across this work is that harnesses encode assumptions about what Claude can't do on its own. However, those assumptions need to be frequently questioned because they can go stale as models improve. As just one example, in prior work we found that Claude Sonnet 4.5 would wrap up tasks prematurely as it sensed its context limit approaching—a behavior sometimes called "context anxiety." We addressed this by adding context resets to the harness. But when we used the same harness on Claude Opus 4.5, we found that the behavior was gone. The resets had become dead weight.

We expect harnesses to continue evolving. So we built Managed Agents: a hosted service in the Claude Platform that runs long-horizon agents on your behalf through a small set of interfaces meant to outlast any particular implementation—including the ones we run today.

Building Managed Agents meant solving an old problem in computing: how to design a system for "programs as yet unthought of." Decades ago, operating systems solved this problem by virtualizing hardware into abstractions—process, file—general enough for programs that didn't exist yet. The abstractions outlasted the hardware. The read() command is agnostic as to whether it's accessing a disk pack from the 1970s or a modern SSD. The abstractions on top stayed stable while the implementations underneath changed freely.

Managed Agents follow the same pattern. We virtualized the components of an agent: a session (the append-only log of everything that happened), a harness (the loop that calls Claude and routes Claude's tool calls to the relevant infrastructure), and a sandbox (an execution environment where Claude can run code and edit files). This allows the implementation of each to be swapped without disturbing the others.

sections.desc: Claude Blog 这篇把 Managed Agents 的 execution 这块继续展开：今天起 Managed Agents 能跑在你控制的 sandbox 里、连到你私有的 MCP server。代码执行、敏感文件、packages、services、数据全留在你的 enterprise 边界里。Cloudflare / Daytona / Modal / Vercel 这几家做 managed sandbox provider。Agent loop 仍然在 Anthropic infra 上跑（orchestration、context 管理、error recovery），tool execution 移到你的环境里。MCP tunnels 是 research preview，让 agent 能穿透防火墙访问你后面的 MCP server。

rewrite: Claude Blog: starting today, Managed Agents can run inside a sandbox you control and connect to private MCP servers. 'Both the sandbox where an agent executes tools and the services it reaches run within the established boundaries of your enterprise, under your security and runtime controls.' Code execution, sensitive files, packages, services, and data stay within your perimeter.

On the split: 'The agent loop that handles orchestration, context management, and error recovery stays on Anthropic's infrastructure, while tool execution moves to your own configured environment.' Self-hosted sandboxes is in public beta; MCP tunnels is in research preview.

On managed providers: 'Choose any sandbox client you want, or start with one of our supported providers: Cloudflare runs sandboxes at scale using microVMs and lighter weight isolates.' The pattern: every time an enterprise asks 'how do we keep our data inside while still using a hosted agent service,' the answer is increasingly some version of this split — orchestration in the vendor's infra, execution in the customer's perimeter, secrets handled by zero-trust injection at the boundary. MCP tunnels is the same shape, just for the network layer instead of the compute layer.

The reasoning: SEO/AEO is the kind of work that benefits most from the new loop pattern — research candidates → test → measure → iterate — and yet most teams still do it manually once a quarter. The weekly autoresearch loop fits exactly into what agentic tools are good at. The fact that it isn't commoditized yet is the alpha window.

On when the alpha dies: 'alpha only gone when we all stop asking these level of questions and start discussion about on-policy autoaeo (does claude optimizing your aeo disproportionately work on claude?) vs generalizable aeo.' Translation: the alpha goes away when the meta-conversation shifts from 'does your autoresearch loop work?' to 'is the loop itself policy-bait for the model vendor?' That's a much later stage of maturity.

sections.title: Steipete：Codexbar icon customization 把他逼疯了，于是让 Codex 自己写了个 editor / Steipete: Codexbar Icon Customization Drove Him Crazy, So He Let Codex Build an Editor for It
sections.desc: Steipete（OpenClaw 创始人）这周把 Codex 当 builder 用出花：第一条讲 Codex 用 browser + computer use 打开 Chrome、进 PR、点 comment、用 macOS picker 上传图片——只为绕开 GitHub 没有 upload API 的限制，他在 VM 里跑 Codex 避免抢 app focus；第二条让大家争 loops 还是 graphs；第三条直接让 Codex 写了个 Codexbar icon 编辑器，因为他自己弄不出来。三条加起来是同一种「Codex 是 build partner 而不是 build replacement」的形状。

rewrite: Peter Steinberger (OpenClaw founder) on Codex doing UI navigation for him: 'It's both amazing and painful to watch codex use browser + computer use to open Chrome, go to my PR, tap on comment and wrangle with the macOS picker — all TO UPLOAD AN IMAGE. GitHub has no API doesn't stop anyone.' The macOS picker — the file dialog that no API exposes — gets driven by Codex clicking through the UI.

On containment: 'I let my codex run in VMs so they don't steal app focus.' Running Codex inside a VM keeps its clicks from stealing focus from whatever he's doing on his actual desktop. The infrastructure pattern: any time an agent gets to drive the cursor, you want it in a sandbox that doesn't interfere with the rest of your work.

The second card carries the loops → graphs frame: 'Are we still talking loops or did we shift to graphs yet?' — a sharp provocation about whether the agentic discussion has moved past linear loops into graph-structured workflows (parallel agents, branching, retries, dependency graphs). Steipete's framing is that the 'loops' era is already feeling dated.

The third card lands the punchline: 'ya'all made me go crazy with codexbar icon customization issues, so I built an editor. (by me, I mean codex).' He let Codex write the editor he couldn't be bothered to write himself — Codex as build partner, not build replacement. The shape: human ships the problem statement, Codex ships the solution.

sections.title: Rauch 把 Sandbox 下载数据免费 + Levie 把「AI 越便宜、生态越大」的逻辑钉出来 / Rauch Frees Sandbox Downloads + Levie Nails "Cheaper AI = Bigger Ecosystem"
sections.desc: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch 周五直接把 Sandbox 数据下载功能 free 出来——「Happy Friday! Time to ship more agents」，把 sandbox 这条 agent 的基础设施管道继续往下推。Box CEO Aaron Levie 转贴的这条把经济学讲清楚：AI 越便宜，整个生态——包括 end-customer——能拿到的机会越大，因为 AI 的瓶颈是「能不能又便宜又稳定地部署到真实 workload 里」，每次把成本压下来，total addressable 的总和就涨一截。

rewrite: Guillermo Rauch (Vercel CEO), Friday: 'Sandbox data for downloads. Happy Friday! Time to ship more agents.' One line; one free feature. The downloads path is now part of the Vercel Sandbox product — agent builders can pull artifacts out of their sandboxes without an additional plumbing step.

The second tweet of the day pairs this with the shipping ethos: 'Ship like @shadcn' — a reference to how shadcn ships small, opinionated, immediately-deployable building blocks. Vercel Sandbox downloads free is the same shape: a small, opinionated, immediately-deployable infrastructure primitive for agents.

Why it matters: yesterday's Vercel AI Gateway token flows dataset (open to all builders) and today's sandbox downloads free are the same playbook — give the agent builder the data + the infra primitives that let them ship something next Monday without paying the vendor tax. Vercel is competing for agent workload on the basis of 'free should-be-commoditizing primitives' rather than pricing the agent runtime.

当天第二条 tweet 把这套和 shipping 哲学绑在一起：「像 @shadcn 那样 ship」——指的是 shadcn 那种 ship 小、有明确观点、能立刻部署的 building block 的方式。Vercel 把 Sandbox downloads 免费开放也是同一个形状：又小、又带观点、又立刻可用的 agent 基础设施 primitive。

为什么重要：昨天的 Vercel AI Gateway token flows dataset（对所有 builder 开放）和今天的 Sandbox downloads 免费，是同一套打法——把 agent builder 下周一就能 ship 东西所需要的数据 + infra primitive 直接给出去，不让 vendor tax 卡住他们。Vercel 在用「免费的、应该被商品化的 primitive」而不是把 agent runtime 卖得更贵——这两条路线之间竞争 agent workload。

rewrite: Aaron Levie (Box CEO), echoing a post: 'This post is key. The cheaper AI gets, the more opportunity there is for the entire ecosystem — especially including end-customers — to benefit. Everything is bottlenecked by being able to successfully and cost effectively deploy AI in real workloads. Any time we can lower the cost of AI, the total addressable market expands.'

The bottleneck framing matters: AI progress isn't bottlenecked by model capability at this point — it's bottlenecked by deployment. The constraint is the same one Sachin flagged in the MAD Podcast: physical compute, power, cooling. Each reduction in cost unlocks a new class of customer that can now afford to use AI in their workload.

The end-customer frame is the underrated part: when AI gets cheaper, it's not just that incumbents ship more features. Whole new categories of customer — small businesses, individual creators, regional enterprises — suddenly become addressable. The asymmetry: cost reductions are multiplicative on TAM, not additive.

sections.title: Madhu Guru：企业做不出 chatbot 之外的 agent，是因为 harness + eval 的人才 gap / Madhu Guru: Enterprises Can't Build Past Chatbots Because of the Harness + Eval Talent Gap
sections.desc: Meta AI Sr Director Madhu Guru 这周连发两条把企业里这一层的真相讲完：为什么很多企业卡在 chatbot 出不来？「人才 gap to build harnesses and evals」。第一条 thread 详细说：evals 你得能清楚表达你的 use case，并且把它变成 offline + online evals；第二条把 Kimi 跟 Google 的关系讲清楚——很多 enterprise 不会直接消费 Kimi，他们会通过 Google Cloud 来消费，因为企业还是要 enterprise 级的 security / data residency / compliance / chip。

cards.authorTag: AI 工程师 / 开源贡献者

rewrite: Madhusudhan (Sr Director of AI at Meta, ex-Google Gemini/Veo/Nano Banana): 'The reason enterprises struggle to go beyond basic chat bots is the talent gap to build harnesses and evals.' The two missing pieces aren't models — they're the harness and the eval.

On evals specifically: 'Evals: do you clearly understand your use cases and can you replicate that in the form of offline and online evals. Do the evals express your ambition and do they push the jagged frontier.' Evals that just confirm the model is OK are easy; evals that capture the jagged frontier of what you want the agent to do are the hard part, and most enterprises don't have people who can write them.

The implication: when Anthropic says Managed Agents decouples session / harness / sandbox so the abstractions can outlive any particular implementation, the buyer they're really courting is the enterprise that has none of the three yet. The value of the abstraction layer is that it compresses the harness + eval talent gap into a hosted service you can call instead of a team you can't hire.

The reason enterprises struggle to go beyond basic chat bots is the talent gap to build harnesses and evals.

Why do you think kimi hurts Google?

Many enterprises won't consume Kimi directly. They'll get it through Google Cloud because they still need enterprise guarantees - security, data residency, compliance and most importantly, chips.

sections.title: Thariq（Claude Code Anthropic 团队）：prototype-first 是避免 token 浪费的最好办法 / Thariq (Anthropic Claude Code team): Prototype-First Is the Best Way to Avoid Wasting Tokens
sections.desc: 前 YC W20 / South Park Commons / Media Lab，现在 Claude Code @Anthropic 的 Thariq 把一个被反复证明的工程心得再讲一次：先 build prototypes of mockups / schemas / data models / proof of concepts，是避免「花了一堆 token 才发现不想要这个 output」最好的办法。这跟 Granola 的 Pedregal「shaping → validate → make it good」流程（上周）是同一个 shape，只是把它简化成了一句 tweet。

cards.authorTag: AI 研究员 / Follow Builders 维护者

rewrite: Thariq (Claude Code at Anthropic, ex-YC W20, ex-South Park Commons, ex-Media Lab): 'building prototypes of mockups, schemas, data models, proof of concepts, etc. is the best way to avoid spending tons of tokens before realizing you don't want the output.' One sentence that lands the whole prototype-first philosophy.

The implicit comparison: the expensive path is to brief the agent with a long spec, let it spend tokens generating the full artifact, then realize at review time that the output shape isn't what you wanted. The cheap path is to spend a small number of tokens on a mockup or schema first, look at it, then either pivot or commit to the full build.

Where it fits in the broader loop pattern: this is the upstream half of the Boris Cherny 'loops > one-offs' frame from earlier this week. Cherny argued for writing lint rules + CI steps so a class of issue gets automated forever. Thariq's point is the dual — at the start of any new build, invest the small token budget to make sure you're building the right class of thing in the first place. Both halves of the same loop.
original: building prototypes of mockups, schemas, data models, proof of concepts, etc. is the best way to avoid spending tons of tokens before realizing you don't want the output

rewrite: Thariq（Anthropic Claude Code 团队，前 YC W20、前 South Park Commons、前 Media Lab）：「build mockup、schema、data model、proof of concept 之类的 prototype，是避免『花了一堆 token 才发现不想要这个 output』最好的办法。」一句话把整个 prototype-first 哲学讲完。
隐含的对比：贵的路径是给 agent 一份长长的 spec，让它花 token 生成完整 artifact，然后在 review 阶段才发现 output 的形状不是你想要的。便宜的路径是先花一小部分 token 做 mockup 或 schema，看完再决定 pivot 还是 commit 完整 build。
它在大 loop 模式里放在哪：这是上周 Boris Cherny 那条「loops > one-offs」frame 的上游半边。Cherny 主张写 lint rule + CI step，让一整类问题永久自动化掉。Thariq 的点是它的对偶——在任何一个新 build 的一开始，先投一笔小的 token 预算，确保你在 build 的本来就是对的那一类。两半是同一个 loop。
original: build mockup、schema、data model、proof of concept 之类的 prototype，是避免「花了一堆 token 才发现不想要这个 output」最好的办法。

sections.title: Weekend texture：会议现在默认录下来给 agent 用 + Nikunj 5:32am + Replit 在下棋 / Weekend Texture: Meetings Now Default-Recorded for Agents + Nikunj at 5:32am + Replit Playing Chess
sections.desc: Weekend fragment 这一块由三条 off-thread 的短观察收尾：Zara Zhang 把「会议默认被录下来」这件事重新框成技术改变文化的样本——不是给 human 看的，而是给 agent 看的；Nikunj 那条 5:32am 父子对话是同一周里少数几条完全非 AI 主题的家庭 moment，放在这里作呼吸；Amasad 给 Replit 社区的 ChessMaxxing 收一笔：chess history 的 exploration 用 Replit 做出来，Replit 社区的下棋文化从这一天开始被命名。

cards.authorTag: Follow Builders 创始人 / 策展人

rewrite: Zhang Rui (Follow Builders founder/curator): 'Just a few years ago, many people were uncomfortable with recording meetings. Now it's just assumed that all business meetings are recorded (not for humans, but for agents). Technology really changes culture.'

The framing is the point: the cultural shift happened not because people wanted recordings for their own sake — they happened because agents need the raw material. Once the agent is the primary consumer, the recording becomes ambient, not invasive. The recorder isn't there to remember for you; it's there to feed the next agent's context.

Pattern to note: this is one of those rare tech-driven cultural shifts where the friction disappeared almost overnight. A few years of 'wouldn't it be weird to record?' to 'of course it's recorded, how else would the agent know what happened?' — same shape as the Git/email/shift, but compressed into ~3 years instead of ~15.
original:
Just a few years ago, many people were uncomfortable with recording meetings

Now it's just assumed that all business meetings are recorded (not for humans, but for agents)

Technology really changes culture

rewrite: Zara Zhang（Follow Builders 创始人 / 策展人）：「几年前，很多人对『录会议』这件事还很不舒服。现在大家默认所有 business meeting 都被录下来——不是给 human 听的，是给 agent 用的。技术真的能改变文化。」
frame 本身就是重点：这种文化转向的发生，不是因为人自己想要录音——是因为 agent 需要原始材料。一旦 agent 才是主要 consumer，录音就变成了 ambient 的，而不是侵入性的。录音机不是替你记忆的，它是为下一个 agent 的 context 供料的。
值得记的模式：这是少数几种技术驱动的文化转向之一——摩擦几乎一夜之间消失。从『录会议怪不怪』到『当然要录，不然 agent 怎么知道发生了什么？』几年就走完了——跟 Git / email 那次转向是同一个形状，但压缩到大概 3 年而不是 15 年。

几年前，很多人对「录会议」这件事还很不舒服。

现在大家默认所有 business meeting 都被录下来——不是给 human 听的，是给 agent 用的。

技术真的能改变文化。

The community's chess obsession has been a slow-building thread for months; this is the moment Masad names it.

The underlying fact: Replit's culture is a surprising testbed for hobby projects because the friction to ship is essentially zero. When a community of builders can spin up a chess-history explorer in an afternoon, naming the pattern ('ChessMaxxing') is what turns a one-off into a movement.
